summary: The condition that leads to pure left-sided heart failure by affecting the aorta’s function is \(\boxed{\text{Aortic stenosis}}\) as it restricts blood flow from the left ventricle without involving other parts of the circulation system significantly. Summary: Primary causes of pure left-sided heart failure include conditions impacting the left ventricular outflow tract; among given options, \( \boxed{\text{aortic stenosis}} \) most accurately fits this criterion because it specifically narrows the aortic valve or related structures, leading exclusively to issues within the left circulatory pathway.

Answer choice: B
